ctx.name: Tom
ctx.notes: Tom moved teams recently and delivery is steady, but Principal-level impact is not visible yet. Previous feedback said he needs early expectation alignment, and he still asks very few questions. I want to test whether he understands what Principal scope means on this team or if he is still operating on old assumptions. I also need to understand whether cross-team trust and judgment calls are developing or whether he is staying in safe execution mode.

focusPoints.label: Principal-level impact and leverage showing up
focusPoints.reason: What’s visible from his Principal scope on this team yet—could be steady delivery without enough leverage, or the impact is happening but not being named or measured clearly.

focusPoints.label: What Principal scope means on this team
focusPoints.reason: Whether he’s operating on the new team’s expectations or old assumptions—worth clarifying what “good” looks like for Principal work here and how he’s interpreting it.

focusPoints.label: Cross-team trust and hard decisions
focusPoints.reason: What he’s doing to build trust beyond his immediate team and make judgment calls visible—Principal work often shows up in how he manages expectations and escalates hard news early.

focusPoints.label: Judgment calls: when to push vs escalate
focusPoints.reason: How they’re calibrating tradeoffs and deciding when to handle vs when to pull you in—at Principal level, the quality of calls is a primary differentiator in performance conversations.

focusPoints.label: Early expectation alignment and question-asking
focusPoints.reason: Whether his “early alignment” is improving—notes suggest he asks very few questions, so it’s worth checking if he’s getting ahead of misunderstandings or still waiting for certainty before engaging.

transcript.question: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
transcript.answer: I think I am shipping, but I am not sure I am aiming at the highest-leverage work yet.

transcript.question: When you say 'highest-leverage work,' what work do you think that is here?
transcript.answer: I can describe Principal impact in general, but this team's bar still feels fuzzy.

transcript.question: describe rincipal impact — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
transcript.answer: I have mostly stayed in my lane instead of forcing cross-team decisions.

transcript.question: Where do you think your leverage is most visible in the work today?
transcript.answer: I have made a few calls independently, but I still over-index on being safe.

transcript.question: When you say 'being safe,' what do you hold back from doing?
transcript.answer: I probably should ask for sharper feedback earlier than I do.

transcript.question: Sharper feedback earlier — what are you usually waiting for before you ask?
transcript.answer: I have not built enough trust with adjacent teams yet.

transcript.question: Where do you think you've fallen short of where you want to be?
transcript.answer: The gap is less technical and more around visible ownership.

transcript.question: What tends to stop you from asking a question early?
transcript.answer: I need explicit expectations for the next cycle so I can calibrate fast.

transcript.question: What would you want me to see differently by our next check-in?
transcript.answer: I will pick one recent deliverable and write down the decision rule I used before our next check-in.
